title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: Set in the Jazz Age on Long Island, this novel depicts narrator Nick Carraway's interactions with mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and Gatsby's obsession with Daisy Buchanan. A timeless story of wealth, class, love, and the American Dream.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship. Teaches how to write clean, maintainable, and efficient code.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: Explores the universe from the Big Bang to black holes, explaining time, space, and cosmology.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A story of racial injustice and moral growth in the American South seen through young Scout Finch.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: Practical advice for improving software development skills and productivity.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A journey through the universe, exploring science, space, and humanity’s place in it.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian novel about surveillance, totalitarianism, and loss of individuality.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: Deep dive into JavaScript concepts like scope, closures, and prototypes.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: Explains evolution from the perspective of genes as the unit of selection.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A mystical journey of a shepherd boy searching for his destiny and treasure.

title: Introduction to Algorithms
author: Thomas H. Cormen
description: Comprehensive textbook on algorithms, data structures, and computational theory.

title: The Origin of Species
author: Charles Darwin
description: Foundational work on evolution by natural selection.
